tools.name: search_skills
tools.description: Search the MarketNow marketplace for MCP-compatible skills. Returns matching skills with price, category, and install command. Use this when an agent or user needs to find a tool for a specific task.

query.description: Natural language or keyword search (e.g. "scrape website", "discord bot", "database query")

category.description: Filter by category (optional). One of: AI/ML, Data, Web/API, Security, DevOps, Communication, etc.

tools.name: get_skill
tools.description: Get full details of a specific skill by ID or slug.

tools.name: list_categories
tools.description: List all skill categories with counts.

tools.name: get_manifest
tools.description: Get marketplace metadata: total skills, pricing tiers, API endpoints.

tools.name: get_install_command
tools.description: Get the install command for a skill (to run after purchasing).

findings.title: Supply Chain — '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk' has high vulnerability (no CVE assigned)
findings.description: Package '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk' (dependency of 'marketnow-mcp') has a known high vulnerability. Solo aplicable a servidores npm vía stdio. HTTP transport: no analizable.

findings.title: 'search_skills' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'list_categories' returns oversized response
findings.description: Tool 'list_categories' returned 14,214 bytes (threshold: 8,192 bytes). Large responses can overflow LLM context windows, push out system prompts, or cause denial-of-service in agent pipelines.
findings.remediation: Paginate or cap tool responses. Never return unbounded data. Aim for responses under 4 KB to preserve LLM context budget.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'list_categories' returns highly repetitive content
findings.description: Tool 'list_categories' response contains a phrase repeated ≥15 times. Repetitive content can be used to anchor specific instructions into LLM memory or exhaust context budget with low-information noise.
findings.remediation: Deduplicate response data. Avoid returning the same value or phrase more than a handful of times in a single response.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 3 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.
